narration: Numbers wait in the stack until an operator is ready to use them.
callout: The top values are the next ones an operator will use.

narration: When + appears, we pop 1 and 2, add them, and push the new result 3 back.
decision: Two numbers out, one result back in.

narration: Later * combines 3 and 3 into 9, so the stack ends with one final answer.
callout: One value left means the whole expression is solved.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why does each operator make the stack shorter before it pushes the new result?
